behavior_summary: U8P_SPOUT::look is a short 'U8P_SPOUT::look' (examine) handler for the SPOUT entity. When the player examines SPOUT it plays a single short bark text "spout " and then the process suspends.
There is no branching, menu interaction, external calls, or flag manipulation — the routine is purely informational and ends by pausing the U8P_SPOUT::look process.
notable_control_flow: Single linear sequence: bark (player-facing text) -> suspend/wait. No conditional branches or menus.
open_questions: Does the engine resume the suspended process automatically after the bark, or is the suspend intended to keep the U8P_SPOUT::look process alive until an external resume?
Is the trailing space in the bark text "spout " intentional or an artifact of extraction?
Are there any game-state effects elsewhere tied to examining SPOUT that are not visible in this short handler?

main_function.name: U8P_SPOUT::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examination response when the player uses the 'U8P_SPOUT::look' action on the SPOUT entity: emit the bark text "spout " then suspend the process.
entry_conditions: Player triggers the 'U8P_SPOUT::look' (examine) action on the SPOUT NPC/object (U8P_SPOUT::look process entry).

function_map.name: U8P_SPOUT::look
function_map.role: U8P_SPOUT::look/examine handler that emits a short bark and suspends
